Name: Charles Johnson, CSP

Charles Johnson is recognized as a seasoned safety professional with a proven track record of making a difference.  Charles has demonstrated his ability to successfully lead both large and small organization through cultural changes in the area of safety and health performance. He has a wife of 27 years with 2 sons and carries a 19 handicap

Name: Chad Cowan
LinkedIn Description:
I am a professional in the Environmental Health and Safety field (25+ years of experience) as well as Human Resources Management (5+ years experience). Both EHS and HR focus on serving others and I embrace this in how I go about my work. I'm proud to say that I've helped my current and previous employers win the Kentucky Governor's Safety and Health Award six times. One of the most rewarding experiences has been re-invigorating  safety cultures and seeing improvements through branding, training enhancements, implementing behavior based safety and driving towards measurement of leading as well as trailing indicators. I have worked 'hands on' in Operations (on the plant floor) as well as worked as a safety trainer/consultant before progressing into management roles. I have been on the ground floor for greenfield startup companies as well as facilities with larger workforces (700+ employees). I have experience managing multi-facility operations and have been responsible for Title V air permitting efforts, Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs), and Hazardous Waste disposal activities. Additionally, I have experience with Japanese automotive (Tier I and II), Chemical Manufacturing, Power Generation/Electrical, Metals (aluminum) and Plastics Manufacturing and in the Amusement/Theme Park industry.  

Currently serve as Corporate Safety and Health Director for Independent Stave Company and am blessed to be part of their amazing team there supplying white oak barrels to the world's finest distillers and vineyards.

 I have coached youth baseball, basketball and softball for 7 years. I also have served on the board for New Beginnings (formerly Rape Victims Services) and have been selected to serve on the Board of Directors for the Kentucky Safety and Health Network (2025-2028) as well as Code Enforcement Board for the City of Eddyville KY.

Name: Melissa Sagastume MBA, SHRM-CP, PHR
LinkedIn Description:
Why would I stay at a company for 15 years? I get to fix the talent and recruiting problems that affect 150 employees and 500+ contractors.  Whether it's training future managers, mentoring new employees, or creating a project plan, I'm there for them. I know every choice talent makes has an impact on someone's life. I want it to be a good one. 

Talent isn't simple or straight-forward. It has to be tailored to the situation and company. We have to start with listening and understanding the why to convince people - candidates or employees - to change. Then, we infuse recruitment automation technology and talent trends that create the best outcome for everyone. 

Every change influences people, and that's why I do this work. I don't take this job lightly. Talent teams convince people to accept new employment so candidates can pursue their joy. If a candidate is willing to take that leap for us? I want to reward that huge career decision with a workplace that's so amazing they want to stay for 15 years. 

If we worked together and you're looking for someone who can build training or technical programs that make your company more efficient and a better place to work, let's talk.

My Approach
In surveying the organizational landscape that individuals, teams or divisions exist in, I am able to see through and challenge assumptions, beliefs, and habits, while inviting everyone along on the change journey. I unearth new perspectives and take risks when the old or comfortable ways no longer serve the organization or the team.  

I guide personal and organizational transformation by promoting self-awareness and encouraging genuine feedback, helping people recognize and shed unproductive behaviors, or simply overplayed strengths.

I believe:
In radical candor
Magic happens outside of the comfort zone
Everyone can become their best at getting better
People become accountable the minute they feel counted
 
Between the lines of a company's talent philosophy is a field where organizations are starting to privilege what it takes to learn over what it takes to change. Out there people are bending their thinking, pushing their paradigms, making mistakes, managing their feelings of awkwardness, and getting on with it. It is a place where I coach people to adopt a growth mindset and they begin to out-behave the competition. 
 
I'll meet you there.

So much of real estate is personal, Carmen says. "It's about building trust, providing value, and keeping lines of communication open during one of the most exciting [and expensive] purchases of their life." 

When asked, Carmen will tell you that she loves what she does and there is nothing in life that she would rather be doing. "My husband and I loved working with Carmen. It's impossible not to let her infectious attitude wrap you up and take you along for the ride," her past client, Polly, has told us. 

If you're even thinking about real estate, Carmen is the place to start. You won't be disappointed. 

Carmen is also a huge supporter of the Cornelia de Lange Syndrome (CdLS) Foundation, a charity that she holds near and dear to her heart. Her daughter passed away in 2015, and a portion of every sale is donated in her honor.

I’m best known as a senior level values-driven Human Resources leader with a strong customer focus. Knowing that what I do everyday makes a difference to a patient is what I find most rewarding about working in the pharmaceutical industry.

As a people manager, I’ve had the privilege of leading teams of 1-30 HR professionals. I have a proven track record of building high performing and well-respected teams.  I love celebrating their achievements, supporting their development, and watching them grow as professionals!

Name: Keith Gauthier
LinkedIn Description:
I grew up tinkering and taking apart everything I could find (and could hide from my parents), reading and being fascinated by nature.  This led me into math and sciences in school where I found a love for data and analytics.  After graduating, I landed what I thought was my dream job in a pharmacology research lab.  After a few years, I realized this was a simply a job for me and not a career.  Around that time, I serendipitously saw an ad to be a counselor to at-risk you in a wilderness camp, applied and have been in the Social Services industry since!! After a couple of years out in the field, I moved into an HR role and that led me to leadership roles in Risk, Safety, HR and Compliance and have been there for the past 18 years!

I love using data to identify areas of improvement and implement process-driven systems, especially when data hasn’t been historically used for that purpose.  From reducing risk and loss to developing staff, fully utilizing available data is often under appreciated and under utilized. 

Outside of work, I spend my free time reading and chasing new experiences, whether that be traveling, trying new foods or experiencing new cultures.  When not on the road, you’ll find me on a trail either hiking, running or biking.  I love to run (as much as anyone can love that kind of torture) and am trying to convince myself to attempt my first 100 miler.
